title: BLKSEQ missed when connecting module port to array
body:
> Thanks for taking the time to report this.
>
> Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

```
module DoesBlockingAssignA (
    input wire clk,
    output reg out
);

    always @(posedge clk)
        out = 1;

endmodule

module DoesBlockingAssignB (
    input wire clk,
    output reg out
);

    always @(posedge clk)
        out = 1;

endmodule

module TestBlockingAssign (
    input wire clk,

    output reg single,
    output reg array [1:0]
);

    DoesBlockingAssignA a( .clk(clk), .out(single) );

    DoesBlockingAssignB b( .clk(clk), .out(array[0]) );

endmodule
```

When this example is run under `verilator -Wall --lint-only`, `BLKSEQ` should be reported for both `DoesBlockingAssignA` and `DoesBlockingAssignB`. However `DoesBlockingAssignB` does not generate a `BLKSEQ` warning.

Specifically I am finding that if you perform a blocking assignment to a module port in a sequential block, and then connect that module port to part of an array, the `BLKSEQ` warning will be missed.

The behavior is still the same using vectors (i.e. `reg [1:0] foo`) rather than arrays (i.e. `reg foo [1:0]`): if the module port is connected to part of it, then `BLKSEQ` is missed.

> What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

I tried the `verilator/verilator:latest` Docker container to get a more recent version. The behavior is still the same. It reports:
```
$ docker run -ti verilator/verilator:latest --version
Verilator 4.203 devel rev v4.202-42-g9699192d
```

> Would you be willing to try to fix Verilator yourself with assistance?

Yes, but I have not contributed before, so it would take some time to familiarize myself with the code.
